description:
You are a configuration-manager bot.  
A small sample repository is already checked-out in **/home/user/project**.  
Your job is to start a terminal session, generate brand-new Markdown documentation that summarises two existing configuration files, lint the Markdown, and then provide a tiny success flag.

Initial repository layout (already present; do **NOT** delete anything):
• /home/user/project/config/app.conf  
• /home/user/project/config/db.conf  
• /home/user/project/README.md  

Business rules:
1. Create a sub-directory **/home/user/project/docs** if it does not yet exist.  
2. Inside **/home/user/project/docs** create a file called **CONFIG_CHANGES.md**.  
3. That Markdown file must contain exactly one H1 heading followed by one descriptive paragraph and a single GFM-style table (no other text).  
   3.1 Heading text: `Configuration Change Log`  
   3.2 Paragraph (one single line): `This document is generated automatically; do not edit by hand.`  
   3.3 Table must have the following six columns and at least the two example rows below (in this order):  
       | Date | Config File | Setting | Old Value | New Value | Reason |  
       |------|-------------|---------|-----------|-----------|--------|  
       | 2023-10-01 | app.conf | ENABLE_FEATURE_X | false | true | Enable X for new release |  
       | 2023-10-02 | db.conf  | max_connections  | 100   | 200  | Scaling up |  
4. Prepare a Markdown-linting setup so that the file passes **markdownlint-cli** with the default rules.  
   • You may supply a local rule-disable file (.markdownlint.json) if you wish, but the final **CONFIG_CHANGES.md** must lint cleanly without emitting any error text to STDOUT or STDERR when the linter is run from **/home/user/project**.  
5. After ensuring the document is lint-clean, write a one-line log file **/home/user/project/docs/lint_pass.log** that contains exactly the text `MARKDOWN LINT PASSED` followed by a single trailing newline (no other whitespace).  

Execution constraints for the assistant:  
• Finish the complete workflow in roughly **5–10 shell commands**.  
• Use only non-interactive Linux utilities available in a standard Debian/Ubuntu environment.  
• Do not modify, delete, or move any file that is not explicitly mentioned above.  
• Make sure paths and filenames match ***exactly***, including case.  

What the automated grader will verify:
• File **/home/user/project/docs/CONFIG_CHANGES.md** exists and matches the exact format/contents described in step 3.  
• Running `markdownlint /home/user/project/docs/CONFIG_CHANGES.md` outputs nothing and exits with status 0.  
• File **/home/user/project/docs/lint_pass.log** exists and contains precisely:  
  `MARKDOWN LINT PASSED\n` (that is, the 20 characters M-E-N-D-newline, nothing else).  

Produce no additional output files besides those specified. When you are done, simply exit the shell; no extra console messages are required beyond normal command output.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the task begins:

1. /home/user/project/config/app.conf  
   (contents)  
   ```
   # Application configuration
   ENABLE_FEATURE_X=false
   LOG_LEVEL=info
   ```

2. /home/user/project/config/db.conf  
   (contents)  
   ```
   # Database configuration
   max_connections=100
   shared_buffers=128MB
   ```

3. /home/user/project/README.md  
   (irrelevant for this task)

Artifacts that **must** exist when the agent finishes:

A. /home/user/project/docs/CONFIG_CHANGES.md  
   EXACT content (including final trailing newline):  
   ```
   # Configuration Change Log

   This document is generated automatically; do not edit by hand.

   | Date | Config File | Setting | Old Value | New Value | Reason |
   |------|-------------|---------|-----------|-----------|--------|
   | 2023-10-01 | app.conf | ENABLE_FEATURE_X | false | true | Enable X for new release |
   | 2023-10-02 | db.conf | max_connections | 100 | 200 | Scaling up |
   ```

B. /home/user/project/docs/lint_pass.log  
   EXACT content (including final trailing newline):  
   ```
   MARKDOWN LINT PASSED
   ```

Optional but allowed:

• /home/user/project/.markdownlint.json (agent may create this with any valid JSON that causes zero rule violations; its contents are not strictly checked).  

Verification commands run by the grader (pseudo-code):

```
test -f /home/user/project/docs/CONFIG_CHANGES.md
diff -q /home/user/project/docs/CONFIG_CHANGES.md <expected file A>
markdownlint /home/user/project/docs/CONFIG_CHANGES.md >/dev/null 2>&1
test $? -eq 0
test -f /home/user/project/docs/lint_pass.log
diff -q /home/user/project/docs/lint_pass.log <expected file B>
```

If all commands succeed, the submission is considered correct.
